report_name: Microsoft Faces Backlash for Threatening Researcher 'Nightmare Eclipse' After Unpatched Zero-Day Disclosures

vulnerabilities.name: BlueHammer
vulnerabilities.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability, named BlueHammer, affecting Windows Defender and BitLocker. It was publicly disclosed by the researcher 'Nightmare Eclipse' and is reportedly being actively exploited.

vulnerabilities.name: RedSun
vulnerabilities.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability, named RedSun, affecting Windows Defender and BitLocker. It was publicly disclosed by the researcher 'Nightmare Eclipse' and is reportedly being actively exploited.

vulnerabilities.name: UnDefend
vulnerabilities.description: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability, named UnDefend, affecting Windows Defender and BitLocker. It was publicly disclosed by the researcher 'Nightmare Eclipse' and is reportedly being actively exploited.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: The vendor of Windows Defender and BitLocker, which publicly threatened legal action against a security researcher for disclosing unpatched zero-day vulnerabilities.

identities.name: Nightmare Eclipse
identities.description: A security researcher who publicly disclosed six unpatched zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft products after alleging mistreatment and having their reporting account revoked by the Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC).

identities.name: Microsoft Security Response Center
identities.description: Microsoft's division for handling vulnerability reports. The researcher Nightmare Eclipse alleged this unit revoked their account, leading to the public disclosure of vulnerabilities.

identities.name: Microsoft Digital Crimes Unit
identities.description: A unit within Microsoft that stated it would pursue legal action against those who enable criminal activity through uncoordinated vulnerability disclosures.

identities.name: Katie Moussouris
identities.description: A prominent security professional who commented on the incident, warning that Microsoft's actions could have a chilling effect on vulnerability research.
